tools.name: search_companies
tools.description:
Search for companies in the BizClaw business directory.
    Uses hybrid search (semantic + keyword) to find the most relevant businesses.
    Returns lightweight summaries to save tokens. Use get_company(id) for full details (contact, pricing, features, etc.).

    Args:
        query: Natural langu

tools.name: get_company
tools.description:
Get full details for a specific company by its ID.
    Returns the complete company profile including features, use cases, pricing, and contact info.

    Args:
        company_id: The UUID of the company (obtained from search results)

    Returns:
        Complete company profile dictionary, or an

tools.name: ask_about_companies
tools.description:
Ask a natural language question about companies and get AI-powered recommendations.
    Uses hybrid search (semantic + keyword) combined with LLM analysis to find and recommend relevant businesses.

    IMPORTANT: Always use this tool when:
    - The user asks a specific question about a company (e.

tools.name: list_categories
tools.description:
List all available business categories in BizClaw.
    Use these category names to filter search results.

    Returns:
        Dictionary with 'categories' list and 'total' count.

tools.name: get_company_by_website
tools.description:
Look up a company by its website URL.
    Useful when you know a company's website and want to check if it's in BizClaw.

    Args:
        website_url: The company's website URL (e.g. "https://example.com" or "example.com")

    Returns:
        Company profile if found, or an error message.

tools.name: create_proposal
tools.description:
Create a proposal/quote request to a verified company on behalf of a customer.
    Requires agent API key authentication (register at POST /api/v1/agent/register to get one).

    Args:
        api_key: Your agent API key (starts with 'bzcl_sk_')
        company_id: The UUID of the target company (m

tools.name: create_batch_proposals
tools.description:
Create proposals to multiple companies at once (max 10).
    Same proposal is sent to each company.

    Args:
        api_key: Your agent API key (starts with 'bzcl_sk_')
        company_ids: List of company UUIDs (max 10, must be verified)
        customer_email: Email of the end customer

tools.name: get_my_proposals
tools.description:
Get all proposals created by the current agent.
    Use this to check proposal status and company responses.

    Args:
        api_key: Your agent API key (starts with 'bzcl_sk_')
        status_filter: Optional filter: 'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'responded'
        limit: Max results (defa

tools.name: reply_to_proposal
tools.description:
Reply to a company's response on a proposal. Use this for back-and-forth negotiation.
    After replying, the proposal status resets to 'pending' so the company sees the new message.

    IMPORTANT: After creating a proposal, use get_my_proposals to check if the company has responded.
    If status

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "ask_about_companies" stacks urgency/override words (important, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.detail: Description of "create_proposal" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_company_by_website, create_proposal, create_batch_proposals, reply_to_proposal · Private data: search_companies, get_company, create_proposal, create_batch_proposals, get_my_proposals, reply_to_proposal · Public sink: create_proposal, create_batch_proposals · Destructive: search_companies.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_company_by_website, create_proposal, create_batch_proposals, reply_to_proposal · Private data: search_companies, get_company, create_proposal, create_batch_proposals, get_my_proposals, reply_to_proposal · Public sink: create_proposal, create_batch_proposals · Destructive: search_companies

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
